1.title: Jeremiah's call
1.text: A priest of Anathoth, Jeremiah prophesied for about 40 years, until Jerusalem's fall.

1.question: According to 1:5, what did God do before forming Jeremiah in the womb?
options: He knew him and ordained him a prophet to the nations

1.question: The mission of 1:10 includes rooting out and pulling down, and also?
What vision does Jeremiah see, facing the north (1:13)?
How does God make Jeremiah before his enemies (1:18)?
options: A fortified city, iron pillar, and walls of bronze

2.title: The broken cisterns
2.text: God recalls Israel's bridal love in the wilderness.

2.question: According to 2:2, what does God fondly remember?
options: The kindness and love of Israel's espousals

2.question: According to 2:11, the people changed their glory for?
What are the two evils of the people (2:13)?
options: They forsook the fountain of living waters and hewed broken cisterns

2.question: According to 2:19, what corrects the people?
How does God say He planted Israel (2:21)?
According to 2:28, Judah's gods were as many as?
According to 2:36, of whom will the people be ashamed, as once of Assyria?

3.title: Return unto me
3.text: Despite the unfaithfulness, God calls the people back.

3.question: How does the people call God in 3:4?
options: My father, the guide of my youth

3.question: According to 3:11, who proved more righteous?
According to 3:16, what will they no longer remember in those days?
What does God promise to heal (3:22)?
What does the people confess in 3:25?
options: That they sinned against the Lord

4.title: Wash thine heart
4.text: Jeremiah pleads for true repentance before the evil from the north.

4.question: From where does the evil God announces come (4:6)?
What did the false prophets say (4:10)?
What must Jerusalem wash to be saved (4:14)?
What does the prophet feel before the war (4:19)?
How did Jeremiah see the earth in the vision (4:23)?
options: Without form and void, and the heavens without light

4.question: What good does it do for Jerusalem to adorn herself (4:30)?

5.title: One just man
5.text: God would seek one just man to pardon the city, but finds none.

5.question: What did God seek in Jerusalem to pardon her (5:1)?
options: One who does justice and seeks the truth

5.question: Why would God not pardon them (5:7)?
options: Because they forsook Him and swore by false gods

5.question: What nation will God bring from afar (5:15)?
options: An ancient, mighty nation of strange tongue

5.question: How is the people described in 5:21?
options: Foolish, with eyes that see not and ears that hear not

5.question: What boundary did God set for the sea (5:22)?
What did the prophets do, and the people love (5:31)?

6.title: The old paths
6.text: The siege draws near; God calls the people back to the good way.

6.question: From where does the evil against Jerusalem appear (6:1)?
How are the people's ears described (6:10)?
What did they say, when there was none (6:14)?
options: 'Peace, peace; when there is no peace'

6.question: What is found by walking the old paths, the good way (6:16)?
How is the people that comes from the north (6:23)?
options: Cruel and without mercy (voice like the sea)

6.question: What shall the people be called (6:30)?

7.title: The temple sermon
7.text: God condemns false trust in the temple while the people sin.

7.question: In what false words did the people trust (7:4)?
options: 'The temple of the Lord, the temple of the Lord'

7.question: What had the Lord's house become in their eyes (7:11)?
What place does God give as an example of what He'll do to the house (7:12)?
To whom did the people make cakes (7:18)?
What did God command the fathers when bringing them from Egypt (7:23)?
What abomination did they do in Topheth (7:31)?
options: Burn their children in the fire

7.question: What shall cease from the cities of Judah (7:34)?
options: The voice of mirth (of bridegroom and bride)

8.title: Is there no balm in Gilead?
8.text: The people persist in deceit and do not repent.

8.question: What do the birds know, but the people do not (8:7)?
options: Their appointed times / the judgment of the Lord

8.question: What did they say, healing the hurt slightly (8:11)?
options: 'Peace, peace; when there is no peace'

8.question: What does God say there is no more of (8:13)?
options: Grapes on the vine nor figs on the fig tree

8.question: What famous question does Jeremiah ask (8:22)?

9.title: The weeping prophet
9.text: Jeremiah wishes to weep without ceasing for his people's ruin.

9.question: To what is the people's tongue compared (9:3)?
What will God make Jerusalem (9:11)?
options: Heaps of stones, a den of dragons

9.question: What will God give this people to eat and drink (9:15)?
Who is called to raise the lament (9:17)?
In what should a man glory (9:24)?

10.title: The idol and the living God
10.text: A contrast between mute idols and the Lord, the everlasting King.

10.question: To what are the idols compared (10:5)?
options: A scarecrow (upright in a cucumber field)

10.question: How is the Lord described, in contrast to idols (10:10)?
options: The living God and the everlasting King (the truth)

10.question: Who made the earth by power and wisdom (10:12)?
Whose it is not to direct his own steps (10:23)?
Why were the flocks scattered (10:21)?
options: Because the shepherds did not seek the Lord

11.title: The broken covenant
11.text: God recalls the covenant the people abandoned.

11.question: Cursed is he who hears not what (11:3)?
Judah's gods were as many as (11:13)?
To what does Jeremiah compare himself, before those who plot against him (11:19)?
options: A lamb brought to the slaughter

12.title: Why do the wicked prosper?
12.text: One of Jeremiah's confessions and God's challenging answer.

12.question: What question does Jeremiah ask God (12:1)?
options: Why does the way of the wicked prosper?

12.question: What image does God use about greater hardships (12:5)?
options: Contending with horses (and the swelling of Jordan)

12.question: What did the people sow and reap (12:13)?
options: They sowed wheat and reaped thorns

12.question: What will God do after plucking up the evil neighbors (12:15)?
options: Have compassion and bring them back

13.title: The linen girdle
13.text: A sign-act about the pride of Judah that will rot.

13.question: What object does Jeremiah hide by the Euphrates (13:1-4)?
What does the rotted girdle represent (13:9)?
options: The pride of Judah (that will rot)

13.question: With what will God fill the inhabitants of the land (13:13)?
What falls from the head of the king and queen (13:18)?
What image shows the people cannot do good (13:23)?
options: The Ethiopian changing his skin, the leopard his spots

14.title: The great drought
14.text: Before the drought, the people cry, but God says not to pray for them.

14.question: What is this word of the Lord about (14:1)?
What does God tell Jeremiah about praying for them (14:11)?
options: Not to pray for this people

14.question: What does God say of the prophets who promise peace (14:14)?
options: That they prophesy falsely; He sent them not

14.question: Who sends rain, according to the people (14:22)?
options: The Lord (not the vanities of the gentiles)

15.title: A wall of bronze
15.text: Not even great intercessors would change the sentence; Jeremiah is strengthened.

15.question: Who, even if interceding, would not change the sentence (15:1)?
How does Jeremiah lament his birth (15:10)?
What did Jeremiah do with God's words (15:16)?
options: Ate them (they were the joy of his heart)

15.question: How does God make Jeremiah against the people (15:20)?

16.title: No marriage, no mourning
16.text: Jeremiah's own life becomes a sign of the imminent judgment.

16.question: What does God forbid Jeremiah (16:2)?
options: To marry and have children (in that place)

16.question: Why would the evil come upon the people (16:11-12)?
options: Because they forsook God and His law

16.question: The new oath will be about God bringing them from where (16:15)?
What will the nations say about the inherited idols (16:19)?
options: That they inherited only lies and vanity

17.title: Blessed is he who trusts in the Lord
17.text: The deceitful heart and the blessing of trust in God.

17.question: Who is blessed, according to 17:7?
options: He who trusts in the Lord

17.question: How is the heart, according to 17:9?
What does Jeremiah ask in 17:14?
options: Healing and salvation (thou art my praise)

17.question: What does God command to keep in 17:21-22?
The blessing for keeping the sabbath: the city will be (17:24-25)?

18.title: The potter and the clay
18.text: At the potter's house, God teaches His sovereignty and the power of repentance.

18.question: The house of Israel is, in God's hand, like (18:6)?
options: The clay in the potter's hand

18.question: If a nation turns, what does God do (18:8)?
options: Relents of the evil He thought to do

18.question: What do the enemies plot against Jeremiah (18:18)?
options: To smite him with the tongue (a plot against him)

18.question: What did they do to Jeremiah, though he interceded for them (18:20)?
options: Dug a pit for him (evil for good)

19.title: The broken bottle
19.text: Jeremiah breaks a bottle in the valley of Hinnom as a sign of ruin.

19.question: What abomination did they do in the high places of Baal (19:5)?
options: Burn their children in the fire

19.question: What does Jeremiah break as a sign (19:10-11)?
Why will God bring the evil on the city (19:15)?
options: Because they hardened their neck, not to hear

20.title: Fire in the bones
20.text: Pashur strikes Jeremiah; the prophet struggles between pain and the word that burns in him.

20.question: Who struck Jeremiah and put him in the stocks (20:1-3)?
What was God's word in Jeremiah like (20:9)?
options: A burning fire shut up in his bones

20.question: What does Jeremiah curse, in his anguish (20:14)?

21.title: Life and death
21.text: Zedekiah inquires of Jeremiah about Babylon; the answer is harsh.

21.question: About whom does Zedekiah inquire of Jeremiah (21:2)?
According to 21:8-9, who goes out and surrenders to the Chaldees?
options: Will live (his life for a prey)

21.question: What does God command the house of David to do (21:12)?
options: Execute justice and deliver the oppressed

22.title: A word to the royal house
22.text: Justice to the oppressed — or ruin for unjust kings.

22.question: What does God command the king to do (22:3)?
Why did the Lord do this to the city (22:9)?
options: Because they forsook the covenant of the Lord

22.question: Woe to him who builds his house with (22:13)?
How shall Jehoiakim be buried (22:19)?
options: With the burial of an ass

22.question: From his youth, what did the people do (22:21)?
options: Never obeyed the voice of God

22.question: What is written of Coniah (Jeconiah) (22:30)?
options: That he is childless (none of his seed will reign)

23.title: The righteous Branch
23.text: Against the false shepherds and prophets, God promises the righteous King.

23.question: What Branch will God raise to David, called 'The Lord Our Righteousness' (23:5-6)?
Of what is the land full (23:10)?
The false prophets speak the vision of (23:16)?
What did the false prophets claim (23:25)?
The true prophet speaks the word with (23:28)?
options: Truth (the chaff is not the wheat)

23.question: God's word is like (23:29)?
options: Fire and a hammer that breaks the rock

23.question: What expression does God forbid to use mockingly (23:33)?

24.title: The two baskets of figs
24.text: The good figs are the exiles; the bad, those who remained.

24.question: What do the good figs represent (24:5)?
options: Judah's captives (whom God will treat for good)

24.question: What do the bad figs represent (24:8-10)?
options: Zedekiah and those who remained (sword, famine, pestilence)

25.title: The seventy years
25.text: Jeremiah announces the captivity and the cup of fury to the nations.

25.question: For how many years did Jeremiah preach unheard (25:3)?
For how many years will the nations serve Babylon (25:11)?
What cup does Jeremiah give the nations (25:15)?
options: The cup of fury (wine of wrath)

25.question: What happens after the seventy years (25:12)?
Who drinks the cup first (25:18)?
options: Jerusalem and the cities of Judah

25.question: How will the Lord manifest Himself against the land (25:30)?
options: He will roar from on high

25.question: Whom does Jeremiah tell to howl, for the days of slaughter have come (25:34)?
To what is the Lord compared, leaving his covert (25:38)?

26.title: Jeremiah's trial
26.text: For the temple sermon, Jeremiah is threatened with death, but acquitted.

26.question: To what place does God compare the house, if they do not obey (26:6)?
What did priests and prophets want to do to Jeremiah (26:8-11)?
What did the princes and people decide (26:16)?
options: That Jeremiah was not worthy of death

26.question: What other prophet was slain by king Jehoiakim (26:20-23)?

27.title: The yoke of Babylon
27.text: Submitting to Babylon is the way of life God offers.

27.question: To whom does God say He gave all these lands (27:6)?
The nation that puts its neck under Babylon's yoke (27:11)?
To whom should the people not listen (27:14-16)?
options: The prophets who say they will not serve Babylon

27.question: Until when will the temple vessels stay in Babylon (27:22)?
options: Until the day God visits them

28.title: Hananiah, the false prophet
28.text: Hananiah contradicts Jeremiah and breaks the yoke; but his word is not from God.

28.question: What does the prophet Hananiah prophesy (28:2-3)?
options: That Babylon's yoke will be broken in two years

28.question: Instead of wooden yokes, God will make (28:13)?
What happened to Hananiah for speaking rebellion (28:16-17)?

29.title: The letter to the exiles
29.text: Jeremiah writes the captives in Babylon a letter of hope.

29.question: What does Jeremiah tell the exiles to do in Babylon (29:5)?
After how many years will God visit the exiles (29:10)?
What thoughts does God have toward the people (29:11)?
options: Of peace, and not of evil (hope)

29.question: How will God be found (29:13)?
options: When they seek Him with all their heart

29.question: Of whom should the people not be deceived in captivity (29:8-9)?
Whom will God punish for prophesying lies unsent (29:31)?

30.title: The Book of Consolation
30.text: God promises to bring Israel and Judah back from captivity.

30.question: According to 30:7, though the day of Jacob's trouble is great, he will be?
Whom will they serve, according to 30:9?
options: The Lord and David their king

30.question: What will God do to the wound that seemed incurable (30:17)?
What covenant formula recurs in 30:22?
options: 'Ye shall be my people, and I will be your God'

30.question: When will the purpose of the Lord's anger be fully understood (30:24)?

31.title: The new covenant
31.text: Jeremiah's central promise: the law written on the heart.

31.question: With what love did God love Israel (31:3)?
Into what will God turn the people's mourning (31:13)?
Who weeps for her children in Ramah (31:15)?
How does God call Ephraim in 31:20?
options: A precious son (the child of His delight)

31.question: Why will each one die, according to 31:30?
Where will God put His law in the new covenant (31:33)?
options: In their inward parts and on their hearts

31.question: In the new covenant, what will God do with sins (31:34)?
options: Forgive and remember them no more

32.title: The purchase of the field
32.text: During the siege, Jeremiah buys a field — an act of faith in restoration.

32.question: Why had Zedekiah imprisoned Jeremiah (32:2-3)?
options: For prophesying the city's surrender to Babylon

32.question: What does Jeremiah buy as a sign of hope (32:9)?
What does the purchase of the field mean (32:15)?
options: Houses and fields shall be bought again in the land (restoration)

32.question: What does Jeremiah declare about God in prayer (32:17)?
options: That nothing is too hard for Him (He made heaven and earth)

32.question: What is already seen against the city (32:24)?
options: The siege mounts of the Chaldees

32.question: How does God identify Himself in 32:27?
options: The Lord, the God of all flesh

32.question: What did the people do to God, according to 32:33?
options: Turned their back to Him, and not the face

32.question: What covenant does God promise to make with the people (32:40)?
options: An everlasting covenant (He will not turn away from them)

32.question: What will they do again in the land (32:44)?
options: Buy fields (God will bring them back from captivity)

33.title: Call unto me
33.text: Still imprisoned, Jeremiah receives the promise of healing and the righteous Branch.

33.question: What does God promise to him who calls on Him (33:3)?
options: To answer and show great and hidden things

33.question: What will God bring to the city, according to 33:6?
options: Health, cure, and abundance of peace

33.question: What Branch of righteousness will God grow to David (33:15-16)?
options: The righteous Branch ('The Lord Our Righteousness')

33.question: The seed of David will be multiplied like (33:22)?
options: The host of heaven and the sand of the sea

33.question: To what is God's faithfulness compared (33:20-25)?
options: The covenant of day and night

34.title: The broken liberty
34.text: The people free the Hebrew slaves and then turn back.

34.question: What does God say to Zedekiah (34:4-5)?
options: That he will die by the sword
That he will not die by the sword, but in peace

34.question: What covenant did Zedekiah make with the people (34:8-9)?
What did they do after freeing the slaves (34:11)?
options: Turned back and enslaved them again

34.question: Since they proclaimed no liberty, God proclaims liberty to (34:17)?
options: The sword, the pestilence, and the famine

35.title: The Rechabites
35.text: The Rechabites' obedience shames Judah's disobedience.

35.question: What did the Rechabites refuse to drink (35:5-6)?
Why do the Rechabites not drink wine or build houses (35:8)?
options: In obedience to their father Jonadab

35.question: The contrast: the Rechabites obeyed their father, but Judah (35:14-15)?
What blessing do the Rechabites receive (35:19)?
options: They will never lack a man before the Lord

36.title: The burned scroll
36.text: The king burns God's word, which is then rewritten and enlarged.

36.question: Who writes Jeremiah's words on the scroll (36:4)?
Why does God command the scroll be written (36:3)?
options: So they may turn and He may forgive

36.question: How did the princes react on hearing the scroll (36:16)?
options: They were afraid and resolved to tell the king

36.question: What do the princes tell Baruch and Jeremiah (36:19)?
What does king Jehoiakim do to the scroll (36:23)?
options: Cuts it and burns it in the brazier

36.question: What does God command Jeremiah after the burning (36:28)?
What did the second scroll have compared to the first (36:32)?
options: The same words and many more besides

37.title: Jeremiah imprisoned
37.text: The illusion of Egypt's help does not change the Lord's word.

37.question: What does God say of Pharaoh's army (37:7)?
options: That it will return to Egypt (not save Judah)

37.question: According to 37:10, even if only wounded men were left, the Chaldees?
options: Would rise and burn the city

37.question: What happens to Jeremiah at the gate of Benjamin (37:13-15)?
options: He is arrested, accused of deserting to the Chaldees

37.question: Where does Zedekiah have Jeremiah placed, with bread (37:21)?
options: In the court of the guard

38.title: The miry cistern
38.text: Jeremiah is cast into a cistern; a foreigner saves him.

38.question: Why do the princes want to kill Jeremiah (38:4)?
options: For weakening the soldiers' hands (his words)

38.question: Who drew Jeremiah out of the cistern (38:7-13)?
What does Jeremiah tell Zedekiah to do to live (38:17)?
options: Surrender to the princes of Babylon

38.question: Whom does Zedekiah fear (38:19)?
options: The Jews who already defected to the Chaldees

38.question: What will become of the king's wives and children if he won't go out (38:23)?
options: They'll be led out to the Chaldees

38.question: Until when did Jeremiah remain in the court of the guard (38:28)?

39.title: The fall of Jerusalem
39.text: The judgment is fulfilled: the city falls and the people are carried to Babylon.

39.question: Where was Zedekiah captured as he fled (39:4-5)?
What did they do to Zedekiah (39:6-7)?
options: Slew his sons and put out his eyes

39.question: To whom did they hand Jeremiah, to protect him (39:14)?

40.title: Gedaliah, the governor
40.text: Babylon leaves Gedaliah over the poor remnant who stayed in the land.

40.question: With whom does Jeremiah go to dwell at Mizpah (40:5-6)?
What does Gedaliah counsel the people to do (40:9)?
options: Not fear to serve the Chaldees and stay in the land

40.question: What warning did Gedaliah not believe (40:14)?
options: That Ishmael wanted to kill him

41.title: The murder of Gedaliah
41.text: Ishmael betrays and kills the governor, plunging the remnant into chaos.

41.question: Why did Ishmael spare ten men (41:8)?
options: Because they had hidden treasures (wheat, honey)

41.question: Who recovered the people Ishmael had taken captive (41:11-14)?
Where do they plan to go, fearing the Chaldees (41:17-18)?

42.title: To ask and disobey
42.text: The remnant asks for guidance, swearing to obey — but had already resolved to go to Egypt.

42.question: What does the people ask of Jeremiah (42:2-3)?
options: To pray to learn the way to go

42.question: What does God promise if they stay in the land (42:10)?
What will happen if they go to Egypt (42:16)?
options: The sword and famine will overtake them there

42.question: How will they die if they insist on going to Egypt (42:22)?

43.title: The flight to Egypt
43.text: The remnant disobeys and takes Jeremiah with them to Egypt.

43.question: What does the people do with Jeremiah's word (43:2-4)?
options: Disobey (accuse him of lying) and go to Egypt

43.question: What sign does Jeremiah make in Egypt (43:9-10)?
options: Hides stones (where Nebuchadnezzar will set his throne)

43.question: What will Nebuchadnezzar do to Egypt (43:11-13)?
options: Smite the land and burn the houses of its gods

44.title: The queen of heaven
44.text: Jeremiah's last great speech denounces the Jews' idolatry in Egypt.

44.question: What did God send the prophets to say (44:4)?
options: Not to do the abomination (incense to other gods)

44.question: Against whom does the people do evil by idolatry (44:7)?
Of those who went to Egypt, who will return to Judah (44:14)?
To whom does the people insist on burning incense (44:17)?
Why did the land become a desert (44:22-23)?
options: Because they burned incense and sinned against the Lord

44.question: What sign does God give: He will give Pharaoh Hophra to (44:30)?

45.title: A word to Baruch
45.text: God comforts Jeremiah's faithful scribe amid his grief.

45.question: What does God say to Baruch about seeking great things (45:5)?
options: Not to seek them; his life will be his prize

46.title: Against the nations
46.text: The oracles against the nations begin, starting with Egypt.

46.question: What day is this by the Euphrates (46:10)?
options: The day of the Lord (of vengeance)

46.question: What is said to the daughter of Egypt (46:11)?
options: That there is no cure for her

46.question: To what is Egypt compared (46:20)?
options: A fair heifer (attacked from the north)

46.question: Whom will God punish in Egypt (46:25)?
options: Amon of Thebes, Pharaoh, and her gods

46.question: To whom does God say 'fear not, for I am with thee' (46:27-28)?

47.title: Against the Philistines
47.text: A brief oracle: the sword of the Lord does not rest.

47.question: Against what people is this prophecy (47:1)?

48.title: Against Moab
48.text: A long oracle against Moab's pride.

48.question: To what is Moab compared, settled and undisturbed (48:11)?
Of what god will Moab be ashamed (48:13)?
The judgment on Moab reaches (48:21-24)?
options: All the cities, far and near

48.question: What ceased from the land of Moab (48:33)?
options: The joy and the wine of the winepresses

48.question: To what is the enemy that comes upon Moab compared (48:40)?
What does God promise Moab in the end (48:47)?

49.title: Against the neighboring nations
49.text: Five brief oracles: Ammon, Edom, Damascus, Kedar/Hazor, and Elam.

49.question: Against whom is the first oracle of chapter 49 (49:1)?
Against what people, descended from Esau, is the prophecy (49:7-10)?
To what will Edom be compared in its destruction (49:18)?
Against what city (with Hamath and Arpad) is the prophecy (49:23)?
Against what eastern peoples is the prophecy (49:28)?
options: Kedar and the kingdoms of Hazor

49.question: What will God break in Elam (49:35)?
What does God promise Elam in the latter days (49:39)?

50.title: Against Babylon
50.text: The book's longest oracle announces the fall of Babylon.

50.question: What will Israel and Judah do when Babylon falls (50:4-5)?
options: Return weeping and seek the Lord

50.question: What does God command His people to do (50:8)?
In that time, Israel's iniquity will be (50:20)?
What is Babylon called, now broken (50:23)?
options: The hammer of all the earth

50.question: Against whom does God declare Himself in 50:31?
To what will Babylon be compared (50:40)?
What happens to the earth at Babylon's capture (50:46)?

51.title: The fall of Babylon
51.text: Babylon's judgment culminates in a sign cast into the Euphrates.

51.question: What does God again command His people (51:6)?
Babylon was, in the Lord's hand, a cup of (51:7)?
Who made the earth by His power (51:15)?
Why is the portion of Jacob (God) different from idols (51:19)?
What will God repay Babylon (51:24)?
options: All the evil she did in Zion

51.question: What happened to Babylon's mighty men (51:30)?
options: They ceased to fight (lost their strength)

51.question: Whom does Jerusalem say devoured her (51:34)?
What sleep will God give those of Babylon (51:39)?
options: A perpetual sleep (from which they won't wake)

51.question: What god will God punish, and what wall will fall (51:44)?
Why will Babylon fall (51:49)?
options: For the slain of Israel she caused

51.question: Though Babylon fortified herself up to heaven (51:53)?
What will happen to Babylon's broad walls (51:58)?
What does Seraiah do with the book against Babylon (51:63-64)?
options: Binds it to a stone and casts it into the Euphrates

52.title: The end of Jerusalem
52.text: A historical appendix that confirms every word of judgment.

52.question: What did they do to Zedekiah (52:10-11)?
options: Slew his sons and put out his eyes

52.question: What did Nebuzaradan burn (52:13)?
options: The house of the Lord (the temple) and the city

52.question: What did the Chaldees break and carry to Babylon (52:17)?
options: The bronze pillars (and the bronze sea of the temple)

52.question: Where were the captured priests and officers slain (52:24-27)?
How many souls in all were carried captive (52:28-30)?
Who was freed and exalted by the king of Babylon (52:31)?
